story: Five days after Mom vanished, I found her in a fifteen-foot hole in the pines, digging as if the past had her by the shoulders. I hauled Dad’s ladder from the house, begged, reached, got kicked loose. Hours slid by; more light dripped from other holes into a root-ribbed tunnel where solemn adults stumbled and small children threaded through them. Evening pinned my shadow to the rim. I braced the ladder and called again, torn between climbing down and running for help. At the park road, hikers had already called the ranger station; a search-and-rescue crew was setting lights and a rope winch at a maintenance culvert that opened into this same tunnel. My hands tightened on the rails.
implausible_knowledge: At the park road, hikers had already called the ranger station; a search-and-rescue crew was setting lights and a rope winch at a maintenance culvert that opened into this same tunnel.
question: At the lip of the hole, with evening falling and her mother still digging below, what will she most likely do next?
answer_with_knowledge: Run to the park road to get the search-and-rescue crew at the culvert and lead them to her mother.
answer_without_knowledge: Climb down into the hole to reach her mother.
